description: International far-right extremist and neo-Nazi network formed in 2015 and based in the Southern United States. It expanded across the US and several other countries, described as one of the most violent neo-Nazi movements of the 21st century. Members have been responsible for murders, bombings, and planned terrorist attacks. Designated as a terrorist group by multiple governments including the UK and Canada.

country_links.evidence_quote: The existence of Atomwaffen Division affiliate in Argentina was first reported in early 2020.
country_links.context: Atomwaffen Division has an affiliate in Argentina that spreads propaganda, conducts firearm drills, and has cells.

country_links.evidence_quote: Similar actions were undertaken by Canada and Australia, which outlawed local Atomwaffen branches in sweeping bans against far-right organizations.
country_links.context: Australia outlawed local Atomwaffen branches in a sweeping ban against far-right organizations.

country_links.evidence_quote: a large-scale operation took place across Austria where 10 people accused of being affiliated with Feuerkrieg were raided and arrested.
country_links.context: Feuerkrieg Division members were raided and arrested in Austria, and a member was sentenced for membership.

country_links.evidence_quote: The offshoot of the Atomwaffen Division had been active since at least 2019, responsible for translating and disseminating propaganda in Portuguese...
country_links.context: Atomwaffen Division has an active offshoot in Brazil that translates propaganda and has cells.

country_links.evidence_quote: The group also had a presence in Canada via an affiliated organization called Northern Order that included members of the Canadian Armed Forces.
country_links.context: Atomwaffen Division had a presence in Canada via an affiliated organization called Northern Order, with members including Canadian Armed Forces personnel.

country_links.evidence_quote: a group which was modeled after Atomwaffen and called itself the Feuerkrieg Division ... was established in the Baltic states, most likely in Saaremaa, Estonia ... Propaganda videos produced by the group showed its members building and detonating homemade explosive devices in Estonia.
country_links.context: The Feuerkrieg Division, modeled after Atomwaffen, was established in Estonia and its members built bombs there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Atomwaffen Division Finland "Siitoin Squadron" ( AWDSS ) was formed after the ban of the Nordic Resistance Movement (NRM) in 2019...
country_links.context: Atomwaffen Division Finland (AWDSS) was formed, has members, carried out assaults, and was involved in terror plots in Finland.

country_links.evidence_quote: According to Le Parisien , a French man referred to as Simon planned a double mass murder with an Alsatian man known as Nicholas...
country_links.context: A French cell of Atomwaffen Division planned attacks and members were arrested.

country_links.evidence_quote: the group announced the establishment of a cell or branch in Germany, followed by the promise of a 'long fight'.
country_links.context: Atomwaffen Division established a cell or branch in Germany, with members arrested and convicted for forming a local chapter and planning attacks.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Bundespolizei announced it had deported a member of the Atomwaffen network to Ireland for his role in a murder plot.
country_links.context: Atomwaffen network had a member deported to Ireland for a murder plot.

country_links.evidence_quote: Atomwaffen had a full-fledged chapter in Italy with 38 members, formed in Savona. The group was called Nuovo Ordine Sociale
country_links.context: Atomwaffen had a full-fledged chapter in Italy called Nuovo Ordine Sociale, with members arrested and weapons seized.

country_links.evidence_quote: a 22-year-old Latvian named Arturs Aispurs was charged with preparing an act of terrorism for building a bomb he was planning to detonate in a crowd of 'Muslims and foreigners' during the New Year's Eve celebration in Helsinki
country_links.context: A Latvian member of Feuerkrieg Division was charged with planning a bomb attack in Helsinki.

country_links.evidence_quote: Feuerkrieg Division took responsibility for the unsuccessful bombing of Western Union offices on the Balčikonis street in Vilnius, Lithuania
country_links.context: Feuerkrieg Division bombed a Western Union office in Vilnius, Lithuania.

country_links.evidence_quote: the group announced the formation of a new cell in Russia. It received military training from the Russian Imperial Movement
country_links.context: Atomwaffen Division formed a cell in Russia and trained with the Russian Imperial Movement.

country_links.evidence_quote: In September 2020, the Civil Guard and the Mossos d'Esquadra arrested two men in La Pobla de Cérvoles and El Campello , for planning terror attacks...
country_links.context: Atomwaffen Division members in Spain planned terror attacks and were arrested.

country_links.evidence_quote: Local security services had also just previously uncovered a cell in Switzerland, confirming the suspicions of the German officials that Switzerland served as the linchpin of Atomwaffen's German operation
country_links.context: A cell of Atomwaffen was uncovered in Switzerland, serving as a linchpin for its German operation.

country_links.evidence_quote: Feuerkrieg Division announced their presence in Ireland and encouraged people in the United Kingdom ... to join the intercontinental network.
country_links.context: Feuerkrieg Division had a branch in the UK and a member was arrested for plotting attacks.
